_meta: Help knowledge base for the on-device keyword 'ask' bot. No AI: src/lib/help-search.ts ranks these by keyword/title overlap with the user's question. 'howto' = using the tool; 'glossary' = BRSR basics. Keep answers short and plain.

topics.title: How do I start a report?
topics.answer:
Start a free readiness report in two steps, all in your browser.
1. **Start a free report**, on the home page, click 'Start a free report'.
2. **Fill the short intake**, client name, industry, size, and their existing compliance filings.

The gap-analysed report is generated instantly, on your device.

topics.title: Why should I add the client's existing filings?
topics.answer:
The 'Existing Compliance Filings' field (PCB, EPR, PAT, ZLD and so on) is what powers the gap analysis.
- **Tick what they file**, and the tool marks the BRSR fields those filings already cover as 'Ready to pull'.
- **No double work**, you don't collect the same data twice.
- **Skip it**, and every field looks like fresh collection.

topics.title: What do Ready, Verify and Collect mean?
topics.answer:
Each BRSR field is colour-coded by status.
- **Ready to pull**, the data already exists in an existing filing.
- **Needs verification**, partly covered, one piece missing.
- **Collect fresh**, not in any filing, gather it new.
- **Not applicable**, a manufacturing-only disclosure hidden for service-sector clients.

topics.title: How do I mark an item as collected?
topics.answer:
Track progress on any field in the Action Plan.
1. **Expand the field**, and use 'Mark as collected'.
2. **Hide collected**, toggle it to focus on what's left.

Your progress is saved on your device, so it survives a refresh or restart.

topics.title: Where is the CSV export?
topics.answer:
Two CSV exports, both on your device.
- **Action Plan → Export CSV**, the full Section A/B/C checklist (status, gap, how-to-collect, collected flags).
- **Materiality → Assessment template**, the suggested topics with empty scoring columns for your stakeholder process.

Everything stays in your browser.

topics.title: What is the Download PDF / data-request brief?
topics.answer:
'Download PDF' makes a clean, structured brief you can send straight to the client's team.
- **Client-ready**, a 'BRSR data request' brief, no Chrome print header or extension overlays.
- **Grouped by owner**, gaps sorted by the team that usually owns each item (HR, EHS, energy manager, company secretary).
- **On-device**, generated in your browser.

topics.title: How do I upload last year's report?
topics.answer:
Carry forward what a returning client already disclosed.
1. **Upload last year's report**, at the top of the Action Plan.
2. **Read on your device**, the PDF never leaves your browser.
3. **'Last year' badges**, disclosures documented last year are tagged, so you know what to carry forward versus collect fresh.

topics.title: How does Suggested Materiality work?
topics.answer:
The Materiality tab suggests ESG topics that matter for the client's industry.
1. **Browse by theme**, topics grouped by Environment, Social and Governance.
2. **Add to shortlist**, pick the ones to carry into the client's stakeholder process.

It is a **starting point, not a finished assessment**, a BRSR-compliant materiality assessment still needs stakeholder engagement.

topics.title: What is the Alignment tab?
topics.answer:
Alignment is the cross-framework crosswalk, collect once, report many.
- **Frameworks**, maps every BRSR disclosure to GRI, TCFD, IFRS S1/S2 and TNFD (nature).
- **Ratings**, plus MSCI and S&P/DJSI rating criteria.

One round of BRSR data serves every other report your client needs.

topics.title: What is Collect / data requests?
topics.answer:
Collect is the Pro tier for chasing BRSR data from a client's team (behind a passcode at /requests).
1. **Add data owners**, each gets a no-login secure link.
2. **Saaksh emails**, a branded request, then auto-reminders, and alerts you on submit.
3. **Auto-compute**, emissions are calculated and a draft is assembled as values come in.

topics.title: Will I lose my work if I close the browser?
topics.answer:
No, your work is saved on your device and survives a close, refresh or restart.
- **What's saved**, form answers, collected ticks, calculator inputs and your materiality shortlist.
- **Coming back**, the home page shows a 'Continue where you left off' banner.
- **The catch**, it is per-device and per-browser; incognito or clearing browser data will erase it, so back it up.

topics.title: Is my client's data stored anywhere?
topics.answer:
It depends on which part of Saaksh you use.
- **Free readiness tool**, no. Everything runs in your browser; nothing is uploaded or stored on a server.
- **Pro Collect tier**, yes, it stores the data you collect (securely), because it has to coordinate a client's team.

The free tool is 100% on-device.

topics.title: How do the emission calculators work?
topics.answer:
The P6 environment fields have built-in calculators for GHG Scope 1 & 2, energy and water.
1. **Enter activity data**, electricity units, diesel litres and so on.
2. **Cited factors**, the tool computes emissions using the CEA grid factor and IPCC/GHG-Protocol fuel factors.
3. **Saved & shared**, inputs persist on your device and carry across the related fields.

topics.title: What is BRSR?
topics.answer: BRSR (Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report) is SEBI's mandatory ESG disclosure for India's top 1,000 listed companies by market cap. It is filed as part of the annual report, ahead of the AGM (so by about 30 September for a March financial year). It has Sections A, B and C across 9 NGRBC principles.

topics.title: What are Sections A, B and C?
topics.answer: Section A is general disclosures (entity identity, operations). Section B is management and process (policies, governance). Section C is the principle-wise performance: 9 principles (P1-P9), each with Essential and Leadership indicators. The gap analysis focuses on Section C; A and B are tracked but not gap-analysed.

topics.title: Essential vs Leadership indicators?
topics.answer: Within each Section C principle, Essential indicators are mandatory for everyone in scope. Leadership indicators are more advanced and voluntary, expected mainly of larger or more mature filers. The tool shows Leadership indicators for listed companies or filers with 3+ years of maturity.

topics.title: What are the 9 NGRBC principles?
topics.answer: BRSR is built on the 9 NGRBC principles: P1 ethics & transparency, P2 product responsibility, P3 employee wellbeing, P4 stakeholder engagement, P5 human rights, P6 environment, P7 policy advocacy, P8 inclusive growth, P9 consumer responsibility.

topics.title: What is materiality / a material topic?
topics.answer: A material topic is an ESG issue that significantly affects the business or matters most to its stakeholders, so it deserves focus and disclosure. A proper BRSR materiality assessment is driven by a stakeholder-engagement process; the tool's shortlist is a starting point for that, not a finished determination.

topics.title: What is BRSR Core and assurance?
topics.answer: BRSR Core is a sub-set of key ESG attributes that must be independently assured. SEBI is phasing in reasonable/limited assurance: top 150 companies first, then 250, 500 and 1,000 over successive years. Assurance means an external provider checks the data, so keep evidence (bills, returns) for every figure.

topics.title: What are Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions?
topics.answer: Scope 1 is direct emissions from owned sources (fuel burned on-site, company vehicles). Scope 2 is indirect emissions from purchased electricity, heat or steam. Scope 3 is all other value-chain emissions (suppliers, business travel, product use). The tool's calculators cover Scope 1 and Scope 2 today.

topics.title: What are GRI, TCFD, IFRS and TNFD?
topics.answer: These are global reporting frameworks BRSR maps to: GRI (broad sustainability standards), TCFD (climate-related financial disclosures), IFRS S1/S2 (ISSB sustainability and climate standards), and TNFD (nature and biodiversity). Mapping lets one round of BRSR data feed reports in all of them.

topics.title: What is CBAM?
topics.answer: CBAM (Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism) is the EU's carbon levy on imports of certain carbon-intensive goods (steel, cement, aluminium, fertiliser and more). Indian exporters to the EU must report the embedded emissions of those goods. It is a planned future Saaksh module.

topics.title: What is CCTS?
topics.answer: CCTS (Carbon Credit Trading Scheme) is India's compliance carbon market, where obligated entities meet emission-intensity targets and trade carbon credits. It is a planned future Saaksh module alongside CBAM and broader ESG.

topics.title: When is BRSR due?
topics.answer: BRSR is filed as part of the annual report, which must be out before the AGM. Listed companies hold the AGM within 6 months of the financial year ending (31 March), so the effective deadline is around 30 September each year. The work mostly happens April to September. It is annual, every covered company refiles each year.

topics.title: What do I do next?
topics.answer:
Here's the sequence from a blank format to a client-ready plan.
1. **Start a free report**, fill the short intake for an instant gap analysis, plus a CSV and a client-ready PDF brief.
2. **Work the Action Plan**, tick items as collected.
3. **Review Materiality & Alignment**, the suggested topics and the cross-framework crosswalk.
4. **Move to Pro (Collect)**, at /requests, create a campaign and add data owners to chase data from the client's team.

topics.title: How do I add a data owner?
topics.answer:
Add data owners from a campaign at /requests/[id].
1. **Open '+ Add a data owner'**, the panel on the campaign page.
2. **Enter name and email**, or pick from your saved contacts for that client.
3. **Choose their BRSR fields**, and send; they get a no-login secure link to a form.

Each owner you add is also saved to that client's contacts, so it's one tap next time.

topics.title: How do I collect BRSR data from a client's team?
topics.answer:
Use Pro (Collect) at /requests to chase data from the client's team.
1. **Create a campaign**, for the client.
2. **Add data owners**, the people who own each set of numbers, and assign each their BRSR fields.
3. **Saaksh chases**, sends a branded request email with a no-login link, alerts you on submit, and auto-reminds anyone pending.
4. **Auto-compute**, as values come in, emissions are calculated and a draft is assembled.

topics.title: What is the assurance / data-ownership ledger?
topics.answer:
The 'Assurance readiness' panel on a campaign turns everything Collect captured into one export.
- **Per data point**, the BRSR code, the value, the named data owner and email, whether evidence was attached, and the cited basis for any computed figure.
- **Download**, a CSV ledger, the evidence trail BRSR Core reasonable assurance now expects.

It only surfaces what was actually collected, **nothing is invented**.

topics.title: How does Collect work?
topics.answer:
Collect is the Pro workflow for collecting BRSR data from a client's team.
1. **Create a campaign**, for one client, add data owners and assign their BRSR fields.
2. **Saaksh emails**, each owner a no-login link; you see live status and they get auto-reminders.
3. **Auto-emissions**, submitted activity data (electricity, diesel) converts to emissions, each figure attributed to who provided it.
4. **Generate outputs**, a grounded BRSR draft plus an assurance-ready ledger.

topics.title: What does Pro give me over the free tool?
topics.answer:
The line is simple: Free is to understand and prepare; Pro is the workspace that does the work.
- **Free (on your device)**, the gap analysis, built-in GHG/energy/water calculators, suggested materiality, cross-framework mapping, AI field guidance on every disclosure, and CSV plus PDF exports.
- **Pro**, Collect (chase data from the client's team), the document auto-fill importer, auto-computed emissions, the assurance ledger, AI narrative draft, and the proposal and fee builder.

topics.title: How do I auto-fill from my documents?
topics.answer:
The import panel on a campaign pre-fills fields from the client's existing report.
1. **Upload the report**, last year's BRSR or annual report.
2. **Read in your browser**, the PDF text is extracted on-device; figures come back as reviewable suggestions, each with the exact source sentence and a confidence badge.
3. **Tick what you trust**, nothing auto-applies; only the ones you tick are saved.

The AI only returns values **literally present** in the document, it never invents.

topics.title: How do I generate the BRSR draft?
topics.answer:
Turn collected data into a draft from the campaign page.
1. **Generate draft**, a deterministic, printable draft of the BRSR responses, grouped by Section and Principle, with the emissions block and a 'nothing is invented' note.
2. **Optional AI narrative**, a short grounded paragraph per principle from the submitted facts, labelled 'AI draft, review before filing'.

The narrative **never invents or alters a number**.

topics.title: What is the CBAM readiness module?
topics.answer:
The free report's 'Beyond BRSR' tab has a CBAM readiness check (and a CCTS one for India).
- **Plain verdict**, likely, may, or unlikely to apply, from the client's industry and export markets.
- **What to prepare**, a dated timeline, a readiness checklist, and cited sources.

It's a **readiness check** to help you prepare, not a liability calculation of embedded emissions.

topics.title: How do I send reminders to owners?
topics.answer:
Saaksh chases pending owners for you.
- **Automatic cadence**, reminders go out a few days apart, up to a few times, with a final nudge near the deadline.
- **Visible status**, each owner card shows when they were last emailed and how many reminders have gone out.
- **Manual nudge**, you can also trigger a reminder to everyone still pending from the campaign page.

topics.title: What is a sample client?
topics.answer:
A sample client is a pre-filled demo campaign so you can see how Collect works first.
- **Ready-made**, it loads a campaign with example owners and fields.
- **Explore freely**, try the owners list, emissions, draft, and assurance ledger before running your own.

It's just for exploring, use a **real campaign** for actual client data.

topics.title: How do I build a proposal and fee?
topics.answer:
The proposal and fee builder (Pro, at /requests/proposal) helps you scope and price an engagement.
1. **Enter scope and your rates**, the client's scope plus your own numbers.
2. **Live breakdown**, a transparent, itemised fee estimate and a downloadable branded proposal PDF.

It structures **your own numbers** and never asserts a market price, so adjust to your market. It runs in your browser and stores nothing.

topics.title: What is Saaksh?
topics.answer:
Saaksh is a compliance tool for independent ESG consultants in India, starting with BRSR.
- **Free readiness tool**, turn a short intake into a gap-analysed, cited BRSR action plan in seconds, all on your device.
- **Pro (Collect)**, the workspace that chases the data from a client's team, computes emissions and drafts the report.

The name comes from the Sanskrit for evidence, every figure is cited or computed, never invented.

topics.title: Is Saaksh free? How much does it cost?
topics.answer:
The readiness tool is **free forever**, no login, nothing stored.
- **Free**, the gap analysis across all 108 fields, all calculators, framework mapping, templates, CBAM/CCTS check, and CSV + PDF exports.
- **Pro (Collect)**, priced per engagement, not a subscription; we onboard consultants manually. See /pricing, or request access.

There is no automated billing yet, so Pro is by request.

topics.title: How do I get Pro / request access?
topics.answer:
Pro (Collect) is onboarded manually for now.
1. **Open the request form**, click any 'Request Pro access' button, or go to /request-pro.
2. **Tell us about your practice**, name, email and roughly how many clients.

We'll set you up and price per engagement. The free tool needs no login and is yours to use meanwhile.

topics.title: Who is Saaksh for?
topics.answer: Saaksh is built for **independent ESG consultants in India** who prepare BRSR reports for their clients, especially those serving a handful of clients who enterprise ESG software prices out. In-house sustainability teams and CAs advising on BRSR use it too.

topics.title: Why is a field 'Not applicable' / product vs services?
topics.answer:
On the intake you pick Product/Manufacturing or Services (auto-guessed from industry, overridable).
- **Services**, certain manufacturing-only disclosures (some P2 and P6 fields) are marked 'Not applicable' and excluded from the gap stats.
- **Change it**, flip Business Type on the form and regenerate if it looks wrong.

Mark a field N/A only with a clear justification for the filing.

topics.title: My client isn't in the company list
topics.answer:
The company box is a typeahead over ~150 Indian listed BRSR filers, purely a convenience.
- **If it's listed**, picking it auto-fills industry and business type (both overridable).
- **If it isn't**, just type the client's name as free text; nothing depends on it being in the list.

The name only labels your report and exports.

topics.title: How do I export the framework mapping?
topics.answer:
On the report's Alignment tab, use **Export (CSV)** to download the crosswalk you're viewing.
- **Framework mapping**, every BRSR disclosure against GRI, TCFD, IFRS S1/S2 and TNFD.
- **Ratings alignment**, the MSCI and DJSI criteria per principle.

It's on-device, so one round of BRSR data can feed all your client's other reports.

topics.title: Is there a Scope 3 calculator in the free tool?
topics.answer:
Yes, the free report has a Scope 3 **screening** calculator on the P6-L2 row (and a standalone /tools/scope3-calculator).
- **Standards-based**, GHG Protocol activity method with cited DEFRA/DESNZ 2024 factors.
- **Covered**, business travel, commuting, transport & distribution, and waste.
- **Deferred**, purchased goods (Category 1) needs supplier/spend data, so it's flagged, not guessed.

It's a screening estimate; Scope 1 & 2 stay the assured figures.

topics.title: What is the Beyond BRSR tab?
topics.answer:
'Beyond BRSR' is a free tab with cited readiness checks for regulations near BRSR.
- **CBAM**, whether your client's EU exports are in scope.
- **CCTS**, whether they're in one of India's notified target sectors.

Each gives a plain verdict, a dated timeline, a checklist and sources. It's a **readiness check, not a liability calculation**.

topics.title: Where are the templates and workbooks?
topics.answer:
The report's **Templates** tab (Reference group) has free, on-device downloadable formats.
- **BRSR response workbook**, all 108 Section C fields plus A and B, as a blank fill-in sheet.
- **Materiality assessment grid** and a **stakeholder engagement plan**, pre-filled starting formats.

They're starting formats to adapt to the client, not finished assessments.

topics.title: Are there how-to guides?
topics.answer: Yes, the **Templates** tab includes short cited how-to guides, for example running a materiality assessment, answering Principle 5 (human rights), getting ready for BRSR Core assurance, and what's new for the current filing year. Longer explainers live on the Saaksh blog (/blog) and the /latest feed.

topics.title: How do I plan the engagement timeline?
topics.answer:
The **Templates** tab has an engagement-timeline generator.
1. **Pick the filing deadline**, defaults to the next 30 September.
2. **First-time or experienced**, choose the plan (20 weeks or 12 weeks).

It lays out the milestones with owners and due dates, working back from the deadline, and exports as a CSV.

topics.title: Where are the internal request emails?
topics.answer: The **Templates** tab has ready internal-request emails, one per owner team (HR, Plant/EHS/Energy, Company Secretary, Procurement, CSR), each personalised with the client name and reporting FY. Click 'Copy email' and paste into Gmail or Outlook. It's the manual version of what **Collect (Pro)** automates: sending, tracking and reminders.

topics.title: Which team owns which BRSR data?
topics.answer: The **Templates** tab has a 'who owns what' map: each BRSR principle mapped to the team that usually holds the data (HR, plant/EHS, energy manager, company secretary, procurement, CSR). It's a reference list plus a CSV download, so you know who to forward each ask to. The Download PDF brief also groups gaps by this owner.

topics.title: What does 'What a complete answer includes' show?
topics.answer: In the **Templates** tab, each Section C field can expand to 'What a complete answer includes', a short note on what an assurance-ready answer covers plus one common gap to avoid, alongside a plain-English explainer. It's guidance on completeness only; the actual figures must come from the client's records, not from illustrative examples.

topics.title: What is 'In plain English' / AI field guidance?
topics.answer: Every Section C field shows a short plain-English explanation of what the disclosure asks for and where a company gets it, tagged as AI-written. It was **pre-generated once** and ships as static text, so there is **no live AI call and nothing about your client is sent anywhere** when you read it.

topics.title: How do I back up or move my work to another device?
topics.answer:
Because work lives only in this browser, you can save it as a file.
1. **Back up work**, in the report sidebar, downloads a small saaksh-backup file.
2. **Restore**, on the Start page, 'Restore your work from a backup file' re-imports it.

This also moves work between browsers or devices. It stays with you, nothing is uploaded.

topics.title: Can I share the report with a colleague?
topics.answer: Yes. In the report sidebar, **Share link** copies a URL that has your intake encoded in it. When a colleague opens it, their browser regenerates the same report locally, nothing is uploaded. Opening a shared link asks before replacing any report you already have open on that device.

topics.title: How do I start a new report / different client?
topics.answer: In the report sidebar, **New report** clears the current session (form, collected ticks and materiality shortlist) and takes you back to a blank intake form for the next client. Back up first if you want to keep the current one. The one-time walkthrough note is kept.

topics.title: How do I change my intake answers?
topics.answer: In the report sidebar, **Back to form** returns to the intake with your answers pre-filled, so you can fix the industry, size, filings or export markets and regenerate. Your collected ticks are kept.

topics.title: Where do the numbers and citations come from?
topics.answer: Everything is cited. The report's **Sources & Methodology** panel lists the knowledge base (ICAI Background Material on BRSR, SEBI circulars, MoEFCC rules) and every emission factor names its publication, table and vintage (CEA grid factor, IPCC/GHG-Protocol, DEFRA). Each disclosure links to the official SEBI BRSR format and its ICAI page.

topics.title: Where do I see regulatory updates?
topics.answer: The **/latest** page is a dated, cited feed of the SEBI, BRSR, CBAM and CCTS moves an Indian consultant should track, alongside the newest guides from the blog. Filter by tag, and each regulatory item links to the primary source. You can subscribe to get these by email.

topics.title: Is there a blog?
topics.answer: Yes, the Saaksh blog (/blog) has practical, cited explainers on BRSR, SEBI regulation, GHG calculations, assurance and sector-specific filing, written for the consultants who file these reports.

topics.title: How do owners attach evidence / bills?
topics.answer: In Collect (Pro), a data owner can attach a supporting document (a bill, invoice or return) per field on their no-login form. Files go to a private store; you view them via a signed link on the campaign, and they're listed in the draft under 'Supporting evidence' for BRSR Core assurance.

topics.title: How do I set the reporting period / financial year?
topics.answer: In Collect (Pro), each campaign carries a BRSR financial year, chosen when you create it (defaulting to the most recently completed year). It appears on the request email, the owner's form, the campaign header and the draft, and you can edit it later from the campaign header.

topics.title: How do I change a campaign's deadline?
topics.answer: On a Collect campaign, use the **Edit** control on the workspace header to change the filing deadline and reporting period. The deadline drives the reminder cadence and the 'final' nudge near the due date.

topics.title: How do I delete a collection / campaign?
topics.answer: On the collections list, the per-card '...' menu has a delete option (it confirms first, and clears the campaign and all its owners and items). Handy for removing test or duplicate campaigns.

topics.title: What is the saved-contacts directory?
topics.answer:
Each Collect campaign (one client) keeps a roster of that client's people.
- **Save once**, by hand or by pasting a block (one per line, split on tab or comma) from an email or spreadsheet.
- **One-tap add**, 'Pick from saved contacts' fills an owner's name and email; you still choose the fields and send.

Every owner you add is auto-captured into the roster too.

topics.title: How do I set my consultant profile / rates?
topics.answer: At /requests/profile you can store your name, firm, contact details and a default rate card, on your device. It seeds the proposal fee builder's rates and brands the proposal PDF with a 'Prepared by' block.

topics.title: What is the Pro dashboard / multi-client view?
topics.answer: The Pro home (/requests) is a dashboard across all your clients: tiles for how many clients, data collected, awaiting and ready-to-draft, plus the collections list. Each client opens into a full workspace with Overview, Auto-fill, Readiness, Owners, Data, Emissions and Draft tabs.

topics.title: How do I see how much data is collected (readiness %)?
topics.answer: In a Collect campaign, the **Readiness** view compares collected values against the full 108 Section C disclosures, showing covered vs missing as a percentage, grouped by Section and Principle, with a 'show only the gaps' toggle. It reflects only real collected values, so an owner submission or auto-fill visibly moves the needle.

topics.title: How do I log in to Collect / I forgot the passcode
topics.answer: Collect (/requests) is gated by a shared passcode set up when you're onboarded. Enter it at /login. If you don't have it yet, request Pro access at /request-pro. Per-consultant accounts are planned; for now it's a single shared passcode.

topics.title: What is the XBRL pre-flight tool?
topics.answer:
The free /tools/xbrl-preflight tool helps you avoid common BRSR XBRL rejections.
- **Converter**, turns Lakh/Crore figures into absolute INR.
- **Checklist**, 7 cited common rejection reasons to check before you upload.

It's a pre-flight aid, not a taxonomy validator.

topics.title: What will the BRSR Core auditor ask for?
topics.answer: The free /tools/audit-readiness tool lists, per KPI, the source documents a BRSR Core reasonable-assurance auditor typically requests, grouped P1 to P9, with the 9 BRSR Core attributes flagged. Filter by principle or 'Core only', and export the whole checklist as a CSV to hand your client.

topics.title: What is the PPP-adjusted intensity tool?
topics.answer: The free /tools/ppp-intensity tool restates an emissions or energy intensity against PPP-adjusted turnover, so an Indian figure compares like-for-like with global peers. It uses the World Bank PPP factor (an editable default) with a vintage caveat.

topics.title: What is the well-being expense schedule tool?
topics.answer: The free /tools/wellbeing-schedule tool maps the 11 BRSR Principle 3 employee-welfare heads to the P&L ledger lines they usually come from, with a cited guide and a downloadable CSV, so the finance team can pull the numbers quickly.

topics.title: Does my client have to file BRSR?
topics.answer: The free /tools/brsr-applicability tool checks whether a company must file BRSR, BRSR Core, or value-chain disclosures for the current year and by when, based on SEBI's top-1000 / top-500 glide path. It's cited to SEBI; verify the current position before advising.

topics.title: What is the materiality matrix builder?
topics.answer: The free /tools/materiality tool is a standalone impact-by-stakeholder-concern grid you can score and export as a CSV, a starting format for the client's stakeholder-driven materiality process. The report's Materiality tab suggests the topics to feed into it.

topics.title: What is BRSR value chain disclosure?
topics.answer: Value chain disclosure is ESG reporting on a company's significant upstream and downstream partners. SEBI's March 2025 circular made it **voluntary now and mandatory from FY 2026-27**, narrowed to partners at 2% or more of purchases or sales. Prepare by identifying those partners early.

topics.title: Assurance vs assessment, what's the difference?
topics.answer: SEBI's March 2025 circular introduced **'assessment'** as a lighter alternative to **reasonable assurance** (under ISAE 3000) for BRSR Core. Both are independent checks of the Core data, but assessment is a less onerous engagement. Which a client can use depends on the year and their category, keep evidence for every figure either way.

topics.title: What is an intensity figure?
topics.answer: An intensity figure normalises an absolute number (like tonnes of CO2e or energy used) by an activity denominator, most often revenue, so you can compare across sizes and years. BRSR asks for several intensities; the tool computes them from your absolute figures and turnover.

topics.title: What is ESG?
topics.answer: ESG stands for Environmental, Social and Governance, the three areas by which a company's sustainability and responsibility are judged. BRSR is India's structured ESG disclosure for listed companies, organised across the 9 NGRBC principles.

topics.title: Which companies must file BRSR?
topics.answer: BRSR is mandatory for India's **top 1,000 listed companies by market capitalisation**. BRSR Core assurance is being phased in from the top 150 down to 1,000 over successive years, and value chain disclosure begins FY 2026-27 for significant partners. Use the applicability tool to check a specific client.

topics.title: Does Saaksh work on mobile / offline?
topics.answer: Saaksh runs in any modern browser, including on a phone or tablet, and the free tool does its work on your device, so once loaded the gap analysis and calculators keep working without a live connection. There's no app to install; just open the site.

topics.title: How do I give feedback or contact you?
topics.answer: Use **Share feedback** at the bottom of this help panel (and in the footer) to tell us what would make Saaksh better, or reach the founder, Rahul Upadhyay, by the email in the footer. Feedback shapes what gets built next.
